name: Phoenix Wright: Ace Attorney Trilogy
description: Become Phoenix Wright and experience the thrill of battle as you fight to save your innocent clients in a court of law. Play all 14 episodes, spanning the first three games, in one gorgeous collection.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • OS: WINDOWS® 10（64bit）,11
  • Processor: Intel® Core™ i3-4160
  • Memory: 4 GB RAM
  • Graphics: Intel® HD Graphics 4400
  • DirectX: Version 11
  • Storage: 3 GB available space
  • Additional Notes: *Recommended Controller Xbox One Wireless Controller (Windows®10/11) For users on Windows®11, please ensure that your device also meets the Windows®11 system requirements.
comment1: The games are all excellent, but what's even more excellent is the amazing work done by the community devoted to making games accessible to the blind and visually impaired. A team of passionate fans developed a wonderful accessibility mod for the trilogy on steam that renders the game fully completable and enjoyable even for a completely blind person, with nearly all gameplay features completely intact or only slightly modified, including investigations. They could've gone the "lazy" way, which would've already been great, of simply allowing a screen reader to "talk" the game's story content, but they went the extra mile to make sure even investigations were playable like a normal person. This is accessibility done right and it should be an example not just for modders, but for devs making games as well.
comment2:
Never played these games when they originally came out. This trilogy is my first time playing them.

These are such wonderful games I regret having never played them sooner. 

The story for each case and the overlying narrative are so intricate and well thought out that you never can guess how things go until the very end. The characters are great and lively, many recurring ones growing throughout the game.

The cases have some very tragic stories and are full of surprises that keep you invested. The world is alive  outside of Phoenix Wright's perspective. There are things going on in the background that you only get glimpses into, just enough to not overwhelm with unnecessary details. Every character has their own motives, plans, and backstory which make them memorable and believable in the game's narrative.

The only issue that can get annoying is how rigid some the puzzles get. There are times when I knew exactly what the solution is but keep getting penalties trying to figure out which specific part of the testimony to match with evidence. It slows the game down when this happens, but luckily it's not very often.

Overall, this is definitely worth playing. Great story. Great characters. It was easily a memorable experience that I cannot recommend enough.
comment3: This game is making me use my brain, which is good. Most days I go blahahsud, when I play this game it makes me feel like einstin.
